# UC-15.1.13 · Transfer Switch Events

## Description

ATS transitions between utility and generator must be logged for root cause of brief outages and equipment stress.

## Value

ATS transitions between utility and generator must be logged for root cause of brief outages and equipment stress.

## Implementation

Ingest dry-contact or SNMP traps on transfer. Alert on failed transfer, oscillation, or long transfer time. Correlate with utility feeder events.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: ATS SNMP/BMS, relay contacts.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="ats:events"` (source, event, duration_ms).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest dry-contact or SNMP traps on transfer. Alert on failed transfer, oscillation, or long transfer time. Correlate with utility feeder events.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=power sourcetype="ats:events"
| where event IN ("transfer_to_gen","retransfer_to_utility","test") OR duration_ms > 500
| table _time, ats_id, event, source_side, duration_ms
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Transfer Switch Events** — ATS transitions between utility and generator must be logged for root cause of brief outages and equipment stress.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="ats:events"` (source, event, duration_ms). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): ATS SNMP/BMS, relay contacts. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: power; **sourcetype**: ats:events. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=power, sourcetype="ats:events". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where event IN ("transfer_to_gen","retransfer_to_utility","test") OR duration_ms > 500` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Transfer Switch Events**): table _time, ats_id, event, source_side, duration_ms
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PDU UI or SNMP poll for the same devices, readings, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (transfer events), Table (last transfer by ATS), Single value (transfers in 24h).

## SPL

```spl
index=power sourcetype="ats:events"
| where event IN ("transfer_to_gen","retransfer_to_utility","test") OR duration_ms > 500
| table _time, ats_id, event, source_side, duration_ms
| sort -_time
```

## Visualization

Timeline (transfer events), Table (last transfer by ATS), Single value (transfers in 24h).
